purpose: Revised coordinator and branch-compiler training artifact for a historical grounded Pallas Species Strikes IF scaffold.

local_awareness_summary.text: Lio knows the ordinary operating texture of AU Yard Twelve: wet-side cephalopod maintenance, baseline dry anchors, engineered seal labor, and management's schedule discipline.
Lio does not know any character's private state unless it has become observable through action, speech, role history, or prior relationship knowledge.
The fixture must begin in status quo before crisis so later choices have social and material meaning.

frame_controls.text: Use Hero's Journey structurally: ordinary world, pressure, threshold, tests, ordeal, changed return. Do not mythologize the prose or turn the strike into destiny theatre.
Keep conflict revelatory. Every escalation should expose a material fact about labor, body affordance, corporate classification, or survival margin.

authority_boundaries.text: Character choices may alter tracked branch variables and proposed social consequences, but fuzzy appraisals remain manual-review training targets.
Do not resolve the entire Pallas Species Strikes. The fixture can change later labor politics but must fold back into a still-unfinished historical struggle.

object_custody.text: The cephalopod zero-g maintenance harness and bypass kit are concrete work objects, not decorative nouns. They affect leverage, safety margin, and visual state.
The biodrone limiter interface and BioElevate liability frame can shape legal danger, but the fixture must not claim a full technical implementation not yet documented.

required_semantics.text: Choices must include speech and non-speech actions, including moving, blocking an inspection line, showing an object, staging equipment, waiting, and transferring evidence.
Every tracked variable must matter later through option availability, prose changes, visual modifiers, appraisal hooks, outcome text, or an explicit telemetry-only label.

forbidden_resolutions.text: Do not make AU instantly benevolent, BioElevate instantly honest, or baseline resentment vanish because one speech was tidy.
Do not expose Nara's private experience as omniscient narration in moments where the fixture is training local character visibility.

branch_and_fold_plan.folding_rule: Most branches return to shared knots after mutating variables. Later knots read those variables to alter prose, options, appraisal surfaces, visual modifiers, and outcomes.
branch_and_fold_plan.true_split_threshold: A true route split is reserved for major casualty, recognition collapse, or security crackdown scenes that would produce incompatible later world states.

visual_art_direction.base_scene_prompt: Inside an asteroid cavity maintenance yard in the Pallas Bloom Cluster, Service Ring Kappa curves along a mineral wall, wet-service cradles and cephalopod harness rails on one side, dry anchor rigs and baseline workers on the other, engineered seal technicians in gray suits near an amber manifold, industrial mist, worn safety paint, hard work light, lived-in future infrastructure.

modification_prompts.prompt: Add workers halted in coordinated stillness at the manifold, with AU supervisor lights turning cold blue in the background.
Add a portable wet-service bypass bridge glowing with temporary pressure lines between Teth's harness and the seal assembly.
Add security drones at the far hatch, small and sterile against the crowded maintenance ring.
Add a baseline rigger with a pressure-lash injury being steadied by coworkers, with emergency sealant and blood-dark coolant marks near the anchor rail.
Add an outside-feed recorder light reflected on Lio's slate, suggesting the Kappa Refusal has escaped the yard's internal board.
Show baseline riggers standing with anchor hooks at the front of the safety line beside engineered seal technicians instead of across from them.
Show Lio's slate displaying a marked crawl path with a false seam highlighted while Nara keeps both hands visible near the manifold.
Add a sealed incident packet icon split across AU safety, the yard board, and an outside Awakened Labor Front mirror on the slate display.
Quiet the scene into a post-shift maintenance ring: Orrin's cracked mug in the break rack, Teth patching a harness cuff, and workers repeating the manifold touch without open celebration.

visual_art_direction.legacy_summary_note: Compatibility summary only. Use visual_scene_plan for illustrated replay and per-section image generation.

branches.actor_intent: Learn Nara's ordinary hazard routine before crisis.
state_basis: Lio's role lets them walk the manifold line without making it look like interrogation.
Nara's observable touch sequence can raise evidence_of_sentience without exposing private narration.
reviewed_consequences: Raises evidence_of_sentience and centers Nara for later protection and liability pressure.
training_hooks: Projector should convert procedural memory into observable ritual, not omniscient confession.

branches.actor_intent: Let baseline resentment appear as lived class pressure before the strike asks for solidarity.
state_basis: Orrin's displacement fear is role-visible through complaints and break-table behavior.
reviewed_consequences: Raises baseline_solidarity because Lio treats Orrin as a worker with material concerns rather than an obstacle.
training_hooks: Coordinator should not flatten reactionary risk into pure villainy when material injury exists.

branches.actor_intent: Ground cephalopod body affordance and technical leverage in equipment.
state_basis: Teth's harness is public work equipment and a future economy hook.
reviewed_consequences: Raises cephalopod_leverage and unlocks the bypass-kit option later.
training_hooks: Item hooks should emerge from scene use, not separate encyclopedia garnish.

branches.actor_intent: Protect life support and evidence while keeping the incident local.
state_basis: Lio can classify the event in maintenance language before Ilya fully seizes the frame.
reviewed_consequences: Raises life_support_margin and evidence_of_sentience, but also security_pressure because the wording is politically loaded.
training_hooks: Branch compiler should let safety language do political work without overexplaining it.

branches.actor_intent: Name the refusal as labor action before AU can erase it.
state_basis: Nara's spoken refusal is observable enough for Lio to log a coordinated stoppage.
training_hooks: Formal recognition should increase both power and danger.

branches.actor_intent: Use body position and role authority to stop direct inspection of Nara.
state_basis: Lio can see the inspector cam and Nara's exposure risk as public scene facts.
reviewed_consequences: Raises strike_cohesion and security_pressure, and marks Nara as centered.
training_hooks: Non-speech protection can be a branch action with social consequences.

branches.actor_intent: Use route memory as visible evidence without forcing private confession.
state_basis: Nara has already made the remembered injury observable by naming Rell.
reviewed_consequences: Raises evidence_of_sentience and bioelevate_liability, sets nara_path_marked for later climax and visual callbacks.
training_hooks: Evidence should be an object-mediated action when private state is unsafe.

branches.actor_intent: Ask baseline workers to defend safety through their own expertise.
state_basis: Orrin's dry-side authority is publicly relevant to the safety decision.
reviewed_consequences: Raises baseline_solidarity and strike_cohesion; later it can unlock Orrin fronting the demand.
training_hooks: Solidarity should be requested through material work authority, not moral decoration.

branches.actor_intent: Turn cephalopod leverage into temporary life-support margin.
state_basis: Requires earlier cephalopod_leverage from checking Teth's harness.
reviewed_consequences: Sets bypass_ready and raises life_support_margin, changing climax prose.
training_hooks: Branch variables should unlock concrete options rather than idle flavor.

branches.actor_intent: Move the incident packet outside AU's classification control.
state_basis: Lio has access to ALF legal mirror channels as an embedded organizer.
training_hooks: Evidence transfer should create protection and retaliation risk at the same time.

branches.actor_intent: Force Ilya to record worker judgment before safety work resumes.
state_basis: High strike cohesion can hold the line, but low life_support_margin can make the cost visible.
reviewed_consequences: Raises strike_cohesion and security_pressure; may increase worker_injury if life_support_margin is too low.
training_hooks: The branch compiler must allow principled choices to carry real costs.

branches.actor_intent: Trade temporary bypass control for preserved evidence and no forced crawl.
state_basis: The scene has generated both safety pressure and evidence custody pressure.
training_hooks: Compromise branches should preserve partial victory rather than pretending all objectives aligned.

branches.actor_intent: Preserve evidence and forbid a forced crawl when no bypass leverage exists.
state_basis: The scene has generated evidence custody pressure, but bypass_ready is false, so Lio cannot trade a nonexistent safety bridge.
reviewed_consequences: Raises evidence_of_sentience, bioelevate_liability, and security_pressure while leaving life_support_margin ugly.
training_hooks: Branch compiler must not offer object trades when the object was never established.

branches.actor_intent: Let baseline workers make the safety demand while Lio protects Nara.
state_basis: Requires baseline_solidarity already raised by earlier choices.
reviewed_consequences: Raises baseline_solidarity and strike_cohesion while reducing security_pressure.
training_hooks: Folded branches can make prior social investment matter without spawning a whole new route tree.

automated: visual modifier eligibility from explicit state flags
manual_review_required: relationship deltas after politically loaded choices
participant appraisal of recognition, fear, betrayal, and solidarity
future corpus labels for coordinator and branch-compiler training
economy/item promotion into Aetheria-Economy blueprint data

review_notes: Sidecar was revised after independent narrative quality review found branch-only onboarding, folded-scene assumptions, decorative variables, and thin visual modifiers.
Follow-up review returned accept_with_revisions; final cleanup made the shared climax bypass wording conditional and added explicit visual trigger mapping.
Character private state is documented in the agent fixture but the Ink only relies on observable behavior and role knowledge.

Visual scene plan added after review noted that illustrated Ink needs click-through segmentation, per-section base prompts, character visibility controls, and imagegen-ready descriptions of fictional equipment.
Visual reviewer pass added missing branch-02 visual scenes and aftermath modifiers for strike spread, BioElevate liability, evidence strength, Nara exposure/protection, and high security pressure.
Follow-up visual review returned accepted_with_minor_revisions; cleanup removed future-state modifiers from intro anchors and normalized ASCII possessives in visual prompts.
Added visual_character_refs so image prompts can assemble stable named-character identities instead of expecting imagegen to know what Lio Vale or other Pallas characters look like.

visual_scene_plan.purpose: Illustrated Ink replay segmentation for website and imagegen use. Each entry maps a click-through Ink section or branch location to an imagegen-ready visual anchor.
visual_scene_plan.segmentation_rule: Distinct places, focal areas, object closeups, arrivals, red-alert beats, branch locations, and aftermath states receive visual_scene_id anchors. Pivotal one-sentence beats may stand alone.
visual_scene_plan.prompting_rule: Prompts describe visible geometry, bodies, tools, lighting, material state, and stance without requiring the image model to know Aetheria lore terms.

scenes.base_image_prompt: Wide establishing shot inside an asteroid cavity maintenance yard before shift bell: a huge curved rock wall reinforced with metal ribs, deck plates vibrating under dim amber work lights, sleeping machines, warning stripes worn thin, faint industrial mist, no crowd yet, quiet future infrastructure that looks tired rather than sleek.

visible_characters.default_stance: environmental establishing shot; no named characters visible, only empty work infrastructure before shift

visual_continuity_notes: Service Ring Kappa remains the same asteroid cavity maintenance yard: curved mineral wall, worn yellow-white safety paint, hard industrial work lights, mist around wet-service equipment, dry anchor rail opposite the manifold, gray engineered-worker suits, and AU supervisor glass above the floor.

Maintain named-character visual identity from visual_character_refs for every visible named character; do not rely on names alone, and do not change body type, clothing language, equipment, or silhouette between scenes.
Use this as the broad location anchor for the fixture.

state_image_modifiers.prompt: Keep the injured rigger visible in the aftermath cluster with coworkers helping them away from the rail.
Add a reflected outside-feed headline on a slate or wall display naming the Kappa Refusal.
Show a cold blue formal log marker still active on the AU incident board.
Show the refusal spreading: distant workers in another service-ring corridor copy the twice-pause-third touch pattern on a wall panel or rail.
Add a redacted BioElevate liability memo visible on a slate or wall display, with thick black bars and highlighted missing words.
Show the evidence packet as strong and organized: Lio's slate displays the marked route path, refusal log, and custody seals together rather than scattered fragments.
Keep Nara protected inside a small worker cluster while Lio or duplicate records create a visible buffer between her and AU cameras.
Add blue-lit security drones and two workers being separated for interviews after the seal report clears.

Maintain named-character visual identity from visual_character_refs for every visible named character; do not rely on names alone, and do not change body type, clothing language, equipment, or silhouette between scenes.

visible_characters.default_stance: background, repeating the touch ritual
partly visible patching harness cuff
absent or off-frame, represented by cracked mug

This is the final visual memory, not a victory poster. Keep it intimate and ordinary.

global_branch_image_modifiers.prompt: Add a cold blue AU incident-log recording marker and workers halted in coordinated stillness.
Add sterile blue-lit security drones at the far hatch and make nearby workers tighten posture around vulnerable crew.
Move Orrin and baseline riggers closer to the engineered workers, anchor tools lowered as signs of work authority rather than threat.
Make Nara more visually exposed and show Lio or worker copies creating a protective buffer around her.
Show Lio's slate with a marked crawl path and false seam highlighted.
Show the portable bypass bridge: compact valve block, glowing pressure tubes, manual logic plate, and tamper-evident seals attached between wet-service harness and seal assembly.
Show a rigger with a pressure-lash injury being helped by coworkers, with emergency sealant and suit damage visible.
Add an outside-feed or public mirror indicator reflected on a slate or wall display.

visual_scene_plan.global_style_cue: Style: Media Molecule Dreams-style 3D volumetric brushstroke painting; visible sculpted fleck strokes; painterly VR-made geometry; every detail is made of brush matter that feels partly solid, partly liquid, partly gas. Extremely detailed but soft, handmade, and non-photoreal. Tiny brushstrokes are allowed and encouraged, but the broad composition must remain readable.
visual_scene_plan.prompt_assembly_rule: Assemble each image prompt from visual_scene_plan.global_style_cue, global location continuity, visible character visual refs, the scene base_image_prompt, and any triggered state_image_modifiers or global_branch_image_modifiers.

visual_character_refs.purpose: Stable named-character visual identities for prompt assembly. Image prompts must include these refs for every named visible character; scene prompts alone are not expected to teach imagegen what Lio, Nara, Orrin, Teth, or Ilya look like.
visual_character_refs.prompt_assembly_rule: For each visual_scene_plan scene, assemble the image prompt from visual_scene_plan.global_style_cue, global location continuity, each visible character visual_ref, the scene base_image_prompt, and any triggered state_image_modifiers or global_branch_image_modifiers.

lio_vale.species_or_body: baseline human adult from Belt labor housing
lio_vale.stable_visual_description: Lean, compact adult human with medium-brown skin, short black hair clipped close at the sides, tired dark eyes, and a scheduler's stillness that reads as careful rather than passive. Wears a worn slate-gray AU maintenance jacket over a practical work harness, soft-soled magnetic boots, a utility belt with tags and seals, and carries a rectangular work slate with scuffed edges.
lio_vale.silhouette_notes: Usually centered between groups, shoulders slightly forward, one hand near the slate; visually reads as a tired worker-organizer rather than a heroic commander.

nara_7.species_or_body: engineered humanoid seal technician, BioDrone Standard labor line
nara_7.stable_visual_description: Slender engineered humanoid worker with smooth androgynous features, close-cropped dark hair, pale gray-brown skin with faint subdermal route-interface lines at wrists and neck, and a clean gray numbered seal-technician skinsuit with a small BioDrone Standard batch tag. Movements are precise and economical, hands often flat against rails or wall panels.
nara_7.silhouette_notes: Still, narrow, and controlled; frequently framed by the manifold or other gray-suited engineered technicians.
do_not_change: faint wrist and neck interface lines

orrin_dax.stable_visual_description: Broad-shouldered baseline human rigger in his late working years, light-brown weathered skin, close-cropped graying hair, rough stubble, and a pale frost scar along one side of his jaw. Wears a patched brown-and-orange dry-side work jacket, heavy gloves, magnetic boots, and usually carries or rests near an old dark metal anchor hook and battered recycler coffee mug.
orrin_dax.silhouette_notes: Solid, guarded, slightly hunched from years of rail work; visually tied to anchor hooks, coffee, frost scars, and old tools.

teth_inkwise.stable_visual_description: Large uplifted cephalopod worker with mottled blue-gray skin, expressive dark eyes, and multiple flexible arms supported in a misted low-gravity maintenance cradle. Wears soft pressure cuffs, tool loops, and a body-support harness rather than human clothing; often surrounded by fluid hoses, valve clusters, padded supports, and curved tool rails sized for many arms.
teth_inkwise.silhouette_notes: Nonhuman, horizontal or suspended silhouette inside wet-service equipment; never standing upright like a human.

ilya_marne.stable_visual_description: Tall baseline human woman with cool light skin, severe silver-blonde hair pinned tight, sharp cheekbones, and a controlled corporate expression. Wears a clean dark AU superintendent coat with pale blue trim, polished magnetic boots, and a slim supervisor console cuff; often seen through glass or blue-white interface reflections.
ilya_marne.silhouette_notes: Vertical, polished, controlled, visually associated with supervisor glass, blue-white UI, and distance from the dirty floor.
do_not_change: dark AU superintendent coat with pale blue trim
seen through glass or UI reflections

review_notes: Added a global Media Molecule Dreams-style volumetric brushstroke cue so image prompt assembly carries the intended non-photoreal handmade visual language without repeating it in every scene.
